1	# Design: Holistic Codebase Consistency Audit
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	Holistic audit of the specdev-cli repo to surface legacy artifacts,
6	contradictions, inconsistencies, duplication, and drift. Findings-only —
7	cleanup is out of scope. Two phases: parallel per-area inventory by
8	general-purpose subagents, then a single synthesis pass in the main session
9	that writes `findings.md`.
10	
11	Motivation: recent assignments reshaped large parts of the repo (00020
12	reduced the test suite; 00022 thinned workflow runtime guidance). Older
13	content (`learnings/`, `docs/plans/`, legacy `distill` helpers, multiple
14	overlapping root docs, and the `templates/.specdev/` ↔ installed `.specdev/`
15	relationship) has not been swept end-to-end. A holistic pass will surface what
16	is stale, contradicted, duplicated, or drifted before it confuses agents or
17	users.
18	
19	Threshold: aggressive — flag anything not actively referenced, even if
20	currently harmless. User triages later.
21	
22	## Scope
23	
24	In scope — five inventory areas:
25	
26	1. **Root docs + misc top-level dirs**
27	   `README.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `QUICKSTART.md`, `SETUP.md`,
28	   `GITHUB_SETUP.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `learnings/`, `docs/`, `docs/plans/`,
29	   `setup-github.sh`, `specdev.assignment-schema.json`.
30	   Looking for: overlap/duplication between user-facing docs, stale
31	   instructions, dirs superseded by `.specdev/knowledge/` or
32	   `.specdev/assignments/`.
33	
34	2. **Source code**
35	   `bin/`, `src/commands/`, `src/utils/`, `scripts/`, `hooks/`.
36	   Looking for: unused exports/functions, commands flagged "legacy" in help
37	   text (e.g. `distill`, `distill done`), dead branches, stale CLI flags,
38	   references to artifacts that no longer exist.
39	
40	3. **Templates + workflow content**
41	   `templates/.specdev/` (source of truth shipped to users) and the installed
42	   `.specdev/_guides/`, `.specdev/_templates/`, `.specdev/_main.md`,
43	   `.specdev/_index.md`.
44	   Looking for: drift between `templates/.specdev/` and the installed
45	   `.specdev/` runtime in this repo (any divergence is a bug since
46	   `specdev update` should keep them aligned), internal contradictions across
47	   guides, references to removed phases/commands.
48	
49	4. **Tests**
50	   `tests/*.js`, `tests/helpers.js`, `tests/test-*-output/` fixture dirs,
51	   `scripts/verify-*.{sh,js}`.
52	   Looking for: orphaned helpers, unused fixtures, tests covering narrow
53	   implementation details (against the 00020 reduced-suite policy), stale
54	   `tmp-*` debug dirs.
55	
56	5. **`.specdev/` project state**
57	   `.specdev/knowledge/`, `.specdev/project_notes/`,
58	   `.specdev/project_scaffolding/`, `.specdev/discussions/`,
59	   `.specdev/assignments/*/status.json` and completed-assignment artifacts.
60	   Looking for: knowledge entries describing replaced behavior,
61	   `project_notes/` claims contradicted by current code, scaffolding that no
62	   longer matches the repo, abandoned/duplicate discussions.
63	
64	Out of scope: `node_modules/`, `package-lock.json`, `.git/`, runtime
65	`.specdev/cache/`, and any fix/cleanup work.
66	
67	## Approach
68	
69	### Phase 1 — Parallel inventory
70	
71	Launch five `general-purpose` subagents in a single message, one per scope
72	area. (`Explore` is unsuitable: it reads excerpts rather than whole files,
73	its own description forbids cross-file consistency checks, and it cannot
74	Write — both of which Phase 1 requires.) Each agent is briefed with:
75	
76	- Its area's file list and any sub-areas to descend into.
77	- The inventory schema below.
78	- The audit threshold ("flag anything not actively referenced, even if
79	  harmless").
80	- A reminder that this is findings-only — do not propose or apply edits.
81	- An explicit instruction to read each in-scope file in full, not in
82	  excerpts, since the audit is a cross-file consistency check.
83	
84	Each agent writes its output to:
85	
86	- `context/inventory-root-docs.md`
87	- `context/inventory-source.md`
88	- `context/inventory-templates.md`
89	- `context/inventory-tests.md`
90	- `context/inventory-specdev-state.md`
91	
92	### Inventory schema
93	
94	Each inventory file follows this shape:
95	
96	```markdown
97	# Inventory: <area>
98	
99	## Files
100	| Path | Purpose (one line) | Last meaningfully touched (commit subject) |
101	
102	## Claims & instructions
103	| Path | Claim/instruction (verbatim or paraphrased) | Where else this is asserted (if known) |
104	(Only for docs/guides/templates/knowledge — skip for pure code.)
105	
106	## References
107	| Path | References these (paths/commands/skills it points to) | Referenced by (paths that mention it) |
108	
109	## Within-area findings
110	| Path | Type (legacy/contradiction/inconsistency/duplication/drift) | Severity | Note | Suggested action |
111	```
112	
113	### Phase 2 — Synthesis (main session)
114	
115	Main session reads all five inventory files together and produces
116	`findings.md` at the assignment root:
117	
118	- Cross-area contradictions become new findings (e.g., `README.md` says X,
119	  `templates/.specdev/_main.md` says Y).
120	- Drift between `templates/.specdev/` and the installed `.specdev/` runtime
121	  is computed by diffing matched paths.
122	- Within-area findings are folded in, grouped by area, with type tags +
123	  severity + suggested action.
124	- A short executive summary at the top: counts by severity, top 5
125	  highest-impact items.
126	
127	## Success Criteria
128	
129	Deliverables:
130	
131	1. Five inventory files under `context/`, one per scope area, conforming to
132	   the schema.
133	2. A single `findings.md` at the assignment root with:
134	   - Executive summary: counts by severity, top 5 highest-impact items.
135	   - One section per scope area, each finding tagged with type
136	     (`legacy / contradiction / inconsistency / duplication / drift`),
137	     severity (`high / med / low`), and a one-line suggested action.
138	
139	Done when:
140	
141	- Every file listed in scope has been read and accounted for in exactly one
142	  inventory.
143	- Every cross-area contradiction the synthesis can detect is recorded
144	  (especially `templates/.specdev/` ↔ `.specdev/` drift, root-docs overlap,
145	  help-text vs implementation drift in `src/commands/`).
146	- Every finding has an action recommendation specific enough that a follow-up
147	  assignment could be opened from it.
148	- No fix or content change is made in this assignment.
149	
150	Verification:
151	
152	- `specdev status` shows the assignment progressed through brainstorm →
153	  breakdown → implementation, with `findings.md` present at the assignment
154	  root.
155	- Spot-check: pick 3 random findings and confirm each cites a specific file
156	  path and concrete recommendation.
157	
158	Non-success:
159	
160	- Generic findings ("docs could be clearer") without a specific path.
161	- Cross-area contradictions only mentioned in one inventory, never reconciled
162	  in `findings.md`.
163	- Cleanup edits sneaking into this assignment.
164	
165	## Key Decisions
166	
167	- **Findings-only output.** Cleanup is queued as follow-up assignments to
168	  keep this assignment scoped and reviewable in one pass.
169	- **Aggressive threshold.** Flag anything not actively referenced. User
170	  triages — easier to drop a flagged item than to retroactively notice an
171	  unflagged one.
172	- **Two-phase orchestration over linear or pure-parallel.** Parallel
173	  inventory keeps the main context lean; a single synthesis pass is needed to
174	  catch cross-area contradictions that per-area agents cannot see.
175	- **Structured inventory schema.** Apples-to-apples comparison across areas
176	  during synthesis. Without a shared shape, cross-area reasoning becomes ad
177	  hoc.
178	- **Per-area inventories live under `context/`.** They are working artifacts
179	  for synthesis, not the deliverable. The deliverable is `findings.md` at the
180	  assignment root.

# Design: Holistic Codebase Consistency Audit

## Overview

Holistic audit of the specdev-cli repo to surface legacy artifacts,
contradictions, inconsistencies, duplication, and drift. Findings-only —
cleanup is out of scope. Two phases: parallel per-area inventory by
general-purpose subagents, then a single synthesis pass in the main session
that writes `findings.md`.

Motivation: recent assignments reshaped large parts of the repo (00020
reduced the test suite; 00022 thinned workflow runtime guidance). Older
content (`learnings/`, `docs/plans/`, legacy `distill` helpers, multiple
overlapping root docs, and the `templates/.specdev/` ↔ installed `.specdev/`
relationship) has not been swept end-to-end. A holistic pass will surface what
is stale, contradicted, duplicated, or drifted before it confuses agents or
users.

Threshold: aggressive — flag anything not actively referenced, even if
currently harmless. User triages later.

## Scope

In scope — five inventory areas:

1. **Root docs + misc top-level dirs**
   `README.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `QUICKSTART.md`, `SETUP.md`,
   `GITHUB_SETUP.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `learnings/`, `docs/`, `docs/plans/`,
   `setup-github.sh`, `specdev.assignment-schema.json`.
   Looking for: overlap/duplication between user-facing docs, stale
   instructions, dirs superseded by `.specdev/knowledge/` or
   `.specdev/assignments/`.

2. **Source code**
   `bin/`, `src/commands/`, `src/utils/`, `scripts/`, `hooks/`.
   Looking for: unused exports/functions, commands flagged "legacy" in help
   text (e.g. `distill`, `distill done`), dead branches, stale CLI flags,
   references to artifacts that no longer exist.

3. **Templates + workflow content**
   `templates/.specdev/` (source of truth shipped to users) and the installed
   `.specdev/_guides/`, `.specdev/_templates/`, `.specdev/_main.md`,
   `.specdev/_index.md`.
   Looking for: drift between `templates/.specdev/` and the installed
   `.specdev/` runtime in this repo (any divergence is a bug since
   `specdev update` should keep them aligned), internal contradictions across
   guides, references to removed phases/commands.

4. **Tests**
   `tests/*.js`, `tests/helpers.js`, `tests/test-*-output/` fixture dirs,
   `scripts/verify-*.{sh,js}`.
   Looking for: orphaned helpers, unused fixtures, tests covering narrow
   implementation details (against the 00020 reduced-suite policy), stale
   `tmp-*` debug dirs.

5. **`.specdev/` project state**
   `.specdev/knowledge/`, `.specdev/project_notes/`,
   `.specdev/project_scaffolding/`, `.specdev/discussions/`,
   `.specdev/assignments/*/status.json` and completed-assignment artifacts.
   Looking for: knowledge entries describing replaced behavior,
   `project_notes/` claims contradicted by current code, scaffolding that no
   longer matches the repo, abandoned/duplicate discussions.

Out of scope: `node_modules/`, `package-lock.json`, `.git/`, runtime
`.specdev/cache/`, and any fix/cleanup work.

## Approach

### Phase 1 — Parallel inventory

Launch five `general-purpose` subagents in a single message, one per scope
area. (`Explore` is unsuitable: it reads excerpts rather than whole files,
its own description forbids cross-file consistency checks, and it cannot
Write — both of which Phase 1 requires.) Each agent is briefed with:

- Its area's file list and any sub-areas to descend into.
- The inventory schema below.
- The audit threshold ("flag anything not actively referenced, even if
  harmless").
- A reminder that this is findings-only — do not propose or apply edits.
- An explicit instruction to read each in-scope file in full, not in
  excerpts, since the audit is a cross-file consistency check.

Each agent writes its output to:

- `context/inventory-root-docs.md`
- `context/inventory-source.md`
- `context/inventory-templates.md`
- `context/inventory-tests.md`
- `context/inventory-specdev-state.md`

### Inventory schema

Each inventory file follows this shape:

```markdown
# Inventory: <area>

## Files
| Path | Purpose (one line) | Last meaningfully touched (commit subject) |

## Claims & instructions
| Path | Claim/instruction (verbatim or paraphrased) | Where else this is asserted (if known) |
(Only for docs/guides/templates/knowledge — skip for pure code.)

## References
| Path | References these (paths/commands/skills it points to) | Referenced by (paths that mention it) |

## Within-area findings
| Path | Type (legacy/contradiction/inconsistency/duplication/drift) | Severity | Note | Suggested action |
```

### Phase 2 — Synthesis (main session)

Main session reads all five inventory files together and produces
`findings.md` at the assignment root:

- Cross-area contradictions become new findings (e.g., `README.md` says X,
  `templates/.specdev/_main.md` says Y).
- Drift between `templates/.specdev/` and the installed `.specdev/` runtime
  is computed by diffing matched paths.
- Within-area findings are folded in, grouped by area, with type tags +
  severity + suggested action.
- A short executive summary at the top: counts by severity, top 5
  highest-impact items.

## Success Criteria

Deliverables:

1. Five inventory files under `context/`, one per scope area, conforming to
   the schema.
2. A single `findings.md` at the assignment root with:
   - Executive summary: counts by severity, top 5 highest-impact items.
   - One section per scope area, each finding tagged with type
     (`legacy / contradiction / inconsistency / duplication / drift`),
     severity (`high / med / low`), and a one-line suggested action.

Done when:

- Every file listed in scope has been read and accounted for in exactly one
  inventory.
- Every cross-area contradiction the synthesis can detect is recorded
  (especially `templates/.specdev/` ↔ `.specdev/` drift, root-docs overlap,
  help-text vs implementation drift in `src/commands/`).
- Every finding has an action recommendation specific enough that a follow-up
  assignment could be opened from it.
- No fix or content change is made in this assignment.

Verification:

- `specdev status` shows the assignment progressed through brainstorm →
  breakdown → implementation, with `findings.md` present at the assignment
  root.
- Spot-check: pick 3 random findings and confirm each cites a specific file
  path and concrete recommendation.

Non-success:

- Generic findings ("docs could be clearer") without a specific path.
- Cross-area contradictions only mentioned in one inventory, never reconciled
  in `findings.md`.
- Cleanup edits sneaking into this assignment.

## Key Decisions

- **Findings-only output.** Cleanup is queued as follow-up assignments to
  keep this assignment scoped and reviewable in one pass.
- **Aggressive threshold.** Flag anything not actively referenced. User
  triages — easier to drop a flagged item than to retroactively notice an
  unflagged one.
- **Two-phase orchestration over linear or pure-parallel.** Parallel
  inventory keeps the main context lean; a single synthesis pass is needed to
  catch cross-area contradictions that per-area agents cannot see.
- **Structured inventory schema.** Apples-to-apples comparison across areas
  during synthesis. Without a shared shape, cross-area reasoning becomes ad
  hoc.
- **Per-area inventories live under `context/`.** They are working artifacts
  for synthesis, not the deliverable. The deliverable is `findings.md` at the
  assignment root.

1	# Codebase Consistency Audit Implementation Plan
2	
3	> **For agent:** Implement this plan task-by-task. The deliverables are markdown
4	> documents (inventories + a findings report), not executable code, so each
5	> task's "test" is a structural conformance check on the produced file, not a
6	> unit test.
7	
8	**Goal:** Produce a findings-only holistic audit of the specdev-cli repo
9	covering legacy artifacts, contradictions, inconsistencies, duplication, and
10	drift across root docs, source, templates, tests, and `.specdev/` state.
11	
12	**Architecture:** Two phases. Phase 1 spawns five parallel general-purpose
13	subagents (one per scope area), each writing a structured inventory file
14	under `context/`. Phase 2 is a single synthesis pass in the main session that
15	reads the five inventories together, detects cross-area contradictions, and
16	writes a single `findings.md` at the assignment root.
17	
18	**Tech Stack:** Markdown. Tooling: SpecDev `Agent` tool, `Read`, `Bash`
19	(`rg`, `find`, `git log`), `Write`/`Edit`.
20	
21	**Execution Mode:** inline
22	
23	---
24	
25	### Task 1: Parallel area inventories
26	
27	**Mode:** full
28	**Skills:** test-driven-development
29	**Files:**
30	- Create: `.specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/inventory-root-docs.md`
31	- Create: `.specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/inventory-source.md`
32	- Create: `.specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/inventory-templates.md`
33	- Create: `.specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/inventory-tests.md`
34	- Create: `.specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/inventory-specdev-state.md`
35	
36	**Step 1: Write the failing structural check**
37	
38	Before spawning agents, write the schema conformance check that all five
39	inventories must pass. The check is a shell snippet that grep-validates each
40	inventory file for the four required H2 sections from the design schema.
41	
42	Save as `.specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/check-inventory-schema.sh`:
43	
44	```bash
45	#!/usr/bin/env bash
46	# Verify every inventory file conforms to the design schema.
47	set -e
48	DIR="$(dirname "$0")"
49	required_sections=("## Files" "## Claims & instructions" "## References" "## Within-area findings")
50	fail=0
51	for f in "$DIR"/inventory-*.md; do
52	  [ -f "$f" ] || { echo "MISSING: $f"; fail=1; continue; }
53	  for s in "${required_sections[@]}"; do
54	    if ! grep -Fq "$s" "$f"; then
55	      echo "FAIL: $f missing section: $s"
56	      fail=1
57	    fi
58	  done
59	done
60	[ $fail -eq 0 ] && echo "ALL INVENTORIES CONFORM"
61	exit $fail
62	```
63	
64	Note: scope area 2 (Source code) is a code area, so the schema allows it to
65	omit "## Claims & instructions". The check tolerates this only for
66	`inventory-source.md`: edit the loop to skip that section for that file. See
67	final form below:
68	
69	```bash
70	#!/usr/bin/env bash
71	set -e
72	DIR="$(dirname "$0")"
73	fail=0
74	for f in "$DIR"/inventory-*.md; do
75	  [ -f "$f" ] || { echo "MISSING: $f"; fail=1; continue; }
76	  required=("## Files" "## References" "## Within-area findings")
77	  case "$(basename "$f")" in
78	    inventory-source.md) ;;
79	    *) required+=("## Claims & instructions") ;;
80	  esac
81	  for s in "${required[@]}"; do
82	    grep -Fq "$s" "$f" || { echo "FAIL: $f missing section: $s"; fail=1; }
83	  done
84	done
85	[ $fail -eq 0 ] && echo "ALL INVENTORIES CONFORM"
86	exit $fail
87	```
88	
89	**Step 2: Run the check — verify it fails (RED)**
90	
91	Run: `bash .specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/check-inventory-schema.sh`
92	Expected: FAIL — output starts with `MISSING:` for all five inventory paths,
93	exit code 1.
94	
95	**Step 3: Spawn the five inventory subagents in parallel**
96	
97	Use a single message containing five `Agent` calls (`subagent_type:
98	general-purpose`), one per area. Each agent receives:
99	
100	- The list of files/dirs in its scope (from `brainstorm/design.md` section
101	  "Scope").
102	- The inventory schema from `brainstorm/design.md` ("Inventory schema"
103	  fenced block).
104	- The output path for its inventory file.
105	- The audit threshold ("flag anything not actively referenced, even if
106	  harmless").
107	- Findings-only reminder.
108	- Explicit instruction to read in-scope files in FULL, not in excerpts.
109	- For `inventory-source.md`: schema allows skipping "## Claims &
110	  instructions".
111	
112	Agent area assignments (paths anchored at repo root
113	`/mnt/h/oceanwave/lib/specdev-cli`):
114	
115	- **inventory-root-docs**: `README.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`,
116	  `QUICKSTART.md`, `SETUP.md`, `GITHUB_SETUP.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`,
117	  `setup-github.sh`, `specdev.assignment-schema.json`, `learnings/**`,
118	  `docs/**`.
119	- **inventory-source**: `bin/**`, `src/commands/**`, `src/utils/**`,
120	  `scripts/**`, `hooks/**`.

# Codebase Consistency Audit Implementation Plan

> **For agent:** Implement this plan task-by-task. The deliverables are markdown
> documents (inventories + a findings report), not executable code, so each
> task's "test" is a structural conformance check on the produced file, not a
> unit test.

**Goal:** Produce a findings-only holistic audit of the specdev-cli repo
covering legacy artifacts, contradictions, inconsistencies, duplication, and
drift across root docs, source, templates, tests, and `.specdev/` state.

**Architecture:** Two phases. Phase 1 spawns five parallel general-purpose
subagents (one per scope area), each writing a structured inventory file
under `context/`. Phase 2 is a single synthesis pass in the main session that
reads the five inventories together, detects cross-area contradictions, and
writes a single `findings.md` at the assignment root.

**Tech Stack:** Markdown. Tooling: SpecDev `Agent` tool, `Read`, `Bash`
(`rg`, `find`, `git log`), `Write`/`Edit`.

**Execution Mode:** inline

---

### Task 1: Parallel area inventories

**Mode:** full
**Skills:** test-driven-development
**Files:**
- Create: `.specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/inventory-root-docs.md`
- Create: `.specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/inventory-source.md`
- Create: `.specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/inventory-templates.md`
- Create: `.specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/inventory-tests.md`
- Create: `.specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/inventory-specdev-state.md`

**Step 1: Write the failing structural check**

Before spawning agents, write the schema conformance check that all five
inventories must pass. The check is a shell snippet that grep-validates each
inventory file for the four required H2 sections from the design schema.

Save as `.specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/check-inventory-schema.sh`:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Verify every inventory file conforms to the design schema.
set -e
DIR="$(dirname "$0")"
required_sections=("## Files" "## Claims & instructions" "## References" "## Within-area findings")
fail=0
for f in "$DIR"/inventory-*.md; do
  [ -f "$f" ] || { echo "MISSING: $f"; fail=1; continue; }
  for s in "${required_sections[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -Fq "$s" "$f"; then
      echo "FAIL: $f missing section: $s"
      fail=1
    fi
  done
done
[ $fail -eq 0 ] && echo "ALL INVENTORIES CONFORM"
exit $fail
```

Note: scope area 2 (Source code) is a code area, so the schema allows it to
omit "## Claims & instructions". The check tolerates this only for
`inventory-source.md`: edit the loop to skip that section for that file. See
final form below:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e
DIR="$(dirname "$0")"
fail=0
for f in "$DIR"/inventory-*.md; do
  [ -f "$f" ] || { echo "MISSING: $f"; fail=1; continue; }
  required=("## Files" "## References" "## Within-area findings")
  case "$(basename "$f")" in
    inventory-source.md) ;;
    *) required+=("## Claims & instructions") ;;
  esac
  for s in "${required[@]}"; do
    grep -Fq "$s" "$f" || { echo "FAIL: $f missing section: $s"; fail=1; }
  done
done
[ $fail -eq 0 ] && echo "ALL INVENTORIES CONFORM"
exit $fail
```

**Step 2: Run the check — verify it fails (RED)**

Run: `bash .specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/check-inventory-schema.sh`
Expected: FAIL — output starts with `MISSING:` for all five inventory paths,
exit code 1.

**Step 3: Spawn the five inventory subagents in parallel**

Use a single message containing five `Agent` calls (`subagent_type:
general-purpose`), one per area. Each agent receives:

- The list of files/dirs in its scope (from `brainstorm/design.md` section
  "Scope").
- The inventory schema from `brainstorm/design.md` ("Inventory schema"
  fenced block).
- The output path for its inventory file.
- The audit threshold ("flag anything not actively referenced, even if
  harmless").
- Findings-only reminder.
- Explicit instruction to read in-scope files in FULL, not in excerpts.
- For `inventory-source.md`: schema allows skipping "## Claims &
  instructions".

Agent area assignments (paths anchored at repo root
`/mnt/h/oceanwave/lib/specdev-cli`):

- **inventory-root-docs**: `README.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`,
  `QUICKSTART.md`, `SETUP.md`, `GITHUB_SETUP.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`,
  `setup-github.sh`, `specdev.assignment-schema.json`, `learnings/**`,
  `docs/**`.
- **inventory-source**: `bin/**`, `src/commands/**`, `src/utils/**`,
  `scripts/**`, `hooks/**`.
- **inventory-templates**: `templates/.specdev/**`, `.specdev/_guides/**`,
  `.specdev/_templates/**`, `.specdev/_main.md`, `.specdev/_index.md`.
- **inventory-tests**: `tests/*.js`, `tests/helpers.js`,
  `tests/test-*-output/**`, `tests/tmp-*/**`, `scripts/verify-*.{sh,js}`.
- **inventory-specdev-state**: `.specdev/knowledge/**`,
  `.specdev/project_notes/**`, `.specdev/project_scaffolding/**`,
  `.specdev/discussions/**`, `.specdev/assignments/*/status.json`,
  completed-assignment artifacts (other phase folders).

**Step 4: Run the check — verify it passes (GREEN)**

Run: `bash .specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/check-inventory-schema.sh`
Expected: PASS — `ALL INVENTORIES CONFORM`, exit code 0.

If any inventory fails the schema check, send the failing agent a follow-up
via `SendMessage` with the exact missing section name; do not respawn from
scratch.

**Step 5: Commit**

```
git add .specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: capture per-area inventories for 00023 audit

Five general-purpose subagents produced structured inventory files
(root docs, source, templates, tests, .specdev state) feeding the
synthesis phase of the codebase consistency audit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

---

### Task 2: Cross-area synthesis and findings report

**Mode:** full
**Skills:** test-driven-development
**Files:**
- Create: `.specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/findings.md`

**Step 1: Write the failing structural check for findings.md**

Save as `.specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/check-findings-schema.sh`:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e
F=".specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/findings.md"
[ -f "$F" ] || { echo "MISSING: $F"; exit 1; }
fail=0
required=(
  "# Findings"
  "## Executive Summary"
  "## Root Docs and Misc"
  "## Source Code"
  "## Templates and Workflow Content"
  "## Tests"
  "## .specdev State"
)
for s in "${required[@]}"; do
  grep -Fq "$s" "$F" || { echo "FAIL: missing section: $s"; fail=1; }
done
# Each finding row must carry type + severity tags.
if ! grep -Eq '\b(legacy|contradiction|inconsistency|duplication|drift)\b' "$F"; then
  echo "FAIL: no finding type tags present"
  fail=1
fi
if ! grep -Eq '\b(high|med|low)\b' "$F"; then
  echo "FAIL: no severity tags present"
  fail=1
fi
[ $fail -eq 0 ] && echo "FINDINGS CONFORMS"
exit $fail
```

**Step 2: Run the check — verify it fails (RED)**

Run: `bash .specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/check-findings-schema.sh`
Expected: FAIL — `MISSING: .specdev/.../findings.md`, exit code 1.

**Step 3: Synthesize findings.md**

Main session reads all five `context/inventory-*.md` files together and
writes `findings.md` with this skeleton:

```markdown
# Findings: Codebase Consistency Audit (00023)
```

Process:

1. Fold each inventory's "Within-area findings" rows into the matching area
   section of `findings.md`, preserving Type / Severity / Note / Suggested
   action.
2. Detect cross-area contradictions by diffing the "Claims & instructions"

     docs.
   - Knowledge or project_notes claims contradicted by current code paths

3. Each cross-area finding goes under `## Cross-Area Findings` with the

4. Fill the Executive Summary by counting rows produced above and selecting

**Step 4: Run the check — verify it passes (GREEN)**

Run: `bash .specdev/assignments/00023_familiarization_codebase-consistency-audit/context/check-findings-schema.sh`
Expected: PASS — `FINDINGS CONFORMS`, exit code 0.

# Findings: Codebase Consistency Audit (00023)

This is a findings-only audit. No fixes are applied. Each finding cites a
specific path, tags type (`legacy / contradiction / inconsistency /
duplication / drift`) and severity (`high / med / low`), and gives a
suggested action specific enough that a follow-up assignment could be opened
from it. Severity labels from per-area inventories that used
`major/moderate/minor` have been normalized to `high/med/low`.

## Executive Summary

**Counts by severity** (within-area + cross-area)

| Severity | Count |
| --- | --- |
| high | 28 |
| med | 36 |
| low | 60 |

**Counts by type**

| Type | Count |
| --- | --- |
| legacy | 47 |
| drift | 39 |
| inconsistency | 22 |
| duplication | 11 |
| contradiction | 5 |

**Top 5 highest-impact items**

1. **`.specdev/` runtime is multiple patches behind `templates/.specdev/`** (drift, high). `_main.md`, `_index.md`, `_guides/workflow.md`, `_guides/assignment_guide.md`, and `_templates/gate_checklist.md` in the installed runtime still describe the old 4-phase workflow with a required Summary phase writing `capture/*-diff.md`, no `specdev next --json`, and required (not optional) Knowledge Capture. Templates have already moved to 3 phases + optional knowledge capture + `workflow.yaml`/`specdev next --json` overlay. Every Claude/Codex session in this repo reads the drifted files. **Action:** `specdev update` (or sync the five files manually) and verify with a `diff -u templates/.specdev/<path> .specdev/<path>` pass.
2. **`docs/plans/` (35 files) is a fossil layer** (legacy, high). None of `src/`, `bin/`, `templates/.specdev/`, or installed `.specdev/` reference any file under `docs/plans/`. Most describe removed commands (`work`/`check`/`ponder`/`remind`/`autoloop`, skills `executing`/`orientation`/`planning`/`scaffolding-lite/full`) or design states already superseded. **Action:** archive whole-sale to `.specdev/knowledge/_archive/plans/` or delete; rely on `.specdev/assignments/<id>/brainstorm/design.md` + `breakdown/plan.md` going forward.
3. **Legacy `distill` chain still wired across the codebase** (legacy, high). `src/commands/distill.js` and `distill-done.js` advertised "Legacy helper" in help text; help/QUICKSTART/CHANGELOG promise `distill workflow`/`distill project` shapes that the CLI does not provide; `continue.js` still emits a `distill_pending` suggestion; utility helpers in `src/utils/scan.js` (`readKnowledgeBranch`, `mark/readProcessedCaptures`) exist only for distill; `tests/test-knowledge.js` keeps the legacy `--assignment=<name>` form alive. **Action:** decide keep-or-drop. If drop: remove the two commands, their dispatcher branches, the `continue.js` block, the scan helpers, the QUICKSTART references, and the CHANGELOG entries.
4. **`scripts/verify-gates.sh` is dead but quoted from three places** (legacy, med). The script asserts V3 artifacts (`scaffold/_architecture.md`, legacy `implementation.md` TDD tables, `review_request.json` with `gate_3/gate_4`) that no current command produces. No caller exists in `package.json`, `hooks/`, `src/`, or `tests/`, but `templates/.specdev/_templates/review_report_template.md` and `.specdev/_templates/review_report_template.md` still instruct reviewers to paste its output, and three `docs/plans/` files reference it. **Action:** delete the script and remove the "Pre-flight (verify-gates.sh)" block in both review_report_template.md copies (or rewrite both around `scripts/verify-assignment-schema.js`, which the maintained test suite actually exercises).
5. **00014 assignment is in a contradictory state and 00022 is uncaptured** (contradiction, high). 00014_feature_agent-friendly-workflow has `implementation/progress.json`, multiple Codex round logs, and `review/implementation-feedback.md`, but its `status.json` records only `brainstorm_approved: true`, and it is missing from `assignment_progress.md`, `feature_descriptions.md`, and `.processed_captures.json`. 00022_refactor_thin-runtime-guidance has both gates approved but is missing from all four state files (`assignment_progress.md`, `feature_descriptions.md`, `.processed_captures.json`, `working_memory.md`). **Action:** decide 00014's true status (abandoned vs. completed-uncaptured) and reconcile; run capture/distill for 00022 (or mark intentionally skipped), then `specdev memory refresh`.

---

## Root Docs and Misc

| Path | Type | Severity | Note | Suggested action |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| AGENTS.md | duplication | high | Near-verbatim duplicate of CLAUDE.md minus the test-confirmation block. Asymmetric content (test-confirmation rule missing) is a drift hazard for Codex sessions. | Pick a single source (e.g., adapter content generated by `init.js`) and have both adapters read identical rules, or add the test-confirmation IMPORTANT block to AGENTS.md. Consider generating both from one template. |
| README.md vs QUICKSTART.md | contradiction | high | README states "3-phase workflow" (Brainstorm/Breakdown/Implement) with optional knowledge capture; QUICKSTART describes "4-Phase Workflow" with explicit "Phase 4: Summary (automatic)" + `capture/*-diff.md` files. `.specdev/_main.md` (installed) says 4 phases; `templates/.specdev/_main.md` says 3. | Reconcile to a single phase count aligned with `templates/.specdev/_main.md`. |
| README.md vs QUICKSTART.md | contradiction | high | README lists `specdev knowledge index/search/list` and never mentions `specdev distill`; QUICKSTART promotes `specdev distill workflow` / `specdev distill project` as primary commands. Current CLI only exposes legacy `specdev distill --assignment=<name>` / `specdev distill done <name>`. | Remove/update the `distill workflow` / `distill project` references in QUICKSTART, or implement those shapes. Tie to the legacy-distill keep/drop decision. |
| README.md vs implementation-speedup design + `_index.md` | contradiction | med | QUICKSTART asserts "two automatic per-task reviews" on every task. After the 2026-02-28 speedup change, `standard` (the default) skips the reviewer subagent. | Soften QUICKSTART to reflect mode-dependent behavior. |
| SETUP.md | legacy | high | v1.0.0-era publish guide; mentions `@specdev/cli` npm publish, template structure (`router.md`, `generic_guides/`, `features/000_example_feature/`) that no longer exists.
